Yogi Bear (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $100,246,011Details
International Box Office $104,528,679
Worldwide Box Office $204,774,690
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,592,492
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,321,593
Total $22,914,085
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A notorious picnic basket thief and general trouble maker, Yogi Bear has always relied on his quick wit and fast feet to stay one step ahead of irate campers while dodging his long-suffering nemesis, Ranger Smith. But he and sidekick Boo Boo are about to face a situation worse than anything Yogi has ever gotten them into… Jellystone Park is being sold! To cover his mismanagement of city funds and fuel his election campaign, Mayor Brown plans to sell the park to loggers. Families will no longer be able to experience the natural beauty of the outdoors Jellystone has always provided—and, even worse, Yogi, Boo Boo, and all their friends will be tossed out of the only home they’ve ever known. Faced with his biggest challenge ever, Yogi must prove he really is “smarter than the average bear,” as he and Boo Boo join forces with Ranger Smith to find a way to save the park from closing forever.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$16,411,322 (16.4% of total gross)
Legs:6.11 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$80,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,515 opening theaters/3,515 max. theaters, 7.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $151,693,488

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 17th, 2010 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: January 21st, 2011 (Wide) (Brazil)
February 11th, 2011 (Wide) (Bulgaria)
Video Release: March 22nd, 2011 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for some mild rude humor.

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A notorious picnic basket thief and general trouble maker, Yogi Bear has always relied on his quick wit and fast feet to stay one step ahead of irate campers while dodging his long-suffering nemesis, Ranger Smith. But he and sidekick Boo Boo are about to face a situation worse than anything Yogi has ever gotten them into… Jellystone Park is being sold! To cover his mismanagement of city funds and fuel his election campaign, Mayor Brown plans to sell the park to loggers. Families will no longer be able to experience the natural beauty of the outdoors Jellystone has always provided—and, even worse, Yogi, Boo Boo, and all their friends will be tossed out of the only home they’ve ever known. Faced with his biggest challenge ever, Yogi must prove he really is “smarter than the average bear,” as he and Boo Boo join forces with Ranger Smith to find a way to save the park from closing forever.
